item.title: clawhq deploy update — push config and version updates to cloud-deployed agents
item.intent: A sovereign cloud agent that cannot be updated is a liability, not an asset. clawhq deploy update must let users push config changes, version upgrades, and new skills to any cloud-deployed agent without reprovisioning. The agent grows pillar must hold for cloud deployments.
item.description: After clawhq deploy provisions a cloud agent, users need to push updates without reprovisioning from scratch. Three update scenarios: (1) config change — update blueprint vars or credentials without restart, (2) clawhq version upgrade — pull latest, rebuild Docker image, restart agent, (3) skill install/remove — evolve the cloud agent remotely. Implementation: SSH keypair injected at provision time (cloud-init). clawhq deploy update --id <agent-id> connects via SSH, pulls new config or runs clawhq update/skill commands on the remote. Requires deploy state file to track SSH endpoint and keypair location. Works in zero-trust mode — user initiates, no inbound from cloud service.
acceptanceCriteria: clawhq deploy update --id <agent-id> --config pushes updated blueprint vars to the remote agent without reprovisioning
clawhq deploy update --id <agent-id> --version pulls latest clawhq, rebuilds Docker image, and restarts the agent on the remote
clawhq deploy update --id <agent-id> --skill install <source> installs a skill on the remote agent
SSH keypair is read from the deploy state file — no manual key management by user
Update fails cleanly if agent-id not found in deploy state — helpful error shown
